cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which of the given statements is sarcastic. Let me start by understanding what sarcasm is. Sarcasm is a form of verbal irony where someone says the opposite of what they mean, often to mock or show contempt. It's usually used to convey a hidden or ironic meaning, often with a tone that's mocking, sardonic, or cutting. Let me look at the two options. Option (A): "One of the worst things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back" Option (B): "One of the enjoyable things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back" First, I'll consider what each statement is saying.
